flow_points: Pakistan started strongly, scoring 64/0 in the Powerplay.
Their Powerplay run rate was an impressive 10.67.
Sahibzada Farhan anchored Pakistan's innings with a century, scoring 100(61).
Farhan's knock was highly impactful, contributing 47.2% of Pakistan's total runs.
D Madushanka claimed 3 crucial wickets for Sri Lanka, conceding 33 runs during Pakistan's innings.
Sri Lanka's chase began with a slower Powerplay, scoring 49/2.
Their Powerplay run rate was 8.17, significantly lower than Pakistan's.
Abrar Ahmed delivered a crucial bowling spell for Pakistan, taking 3 wickets for 26 runs.
Sri Lanka's high-scoring finish in Death Overs (14.4 RR) created a significant tactical turning point, shifting momentum.
Despite this late surge, Pakistan ultimately won the match by 5 runs.
